page: Microsoft Azure L32s_v2 is a storage-optimized virtual machine from the LSv2 family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7551 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of 8008 GB of local storage, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPU accelerators are present on this server type.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile characterized by a tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively low, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10%, and single-threaded gzip and bzip3 operations categorized as weak. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression benchmarks. Memory bandwidth remains stable in the average tier, reaching 70.9 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 36.37 GB/sec for write operations.

With its massive 8008 GB local storage capacity and 256.0 GB of RAM, the L32s_v2 is highly resource-dense, offering qualitative cost efficiency for storage-heavy and database workloads that require large local scratch space or high-capacity caching. It achieves average performance in database operations, with Redis SET operations reaching 18.21 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 12.95 GB/sec. The server is well-suited for large databases, big data analytics, and high-throughput web serving, though it is less optimal for compute-bound or single-threaded tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure L32s_v2 is an LSv2-family virtual machine designed for storage-intensive workloads. Powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7551 processor running at 3.2 GHz, it provides 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM. The server features a substantial 8008 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff: single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance are weak, while multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform at average levels. For example, Redis SET operations reach 18.21 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is 12.95 GB/sec. This hardware profile offers high resource density, making it qualitatively cost-effective for large databases, big data processing, and caching applications that require massive local storage capacity rather than high single-threaded compute performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L32s_v2 is an LSv2 virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 8008 GB of local storage. It offers average multi-core performance and is optimized for large databases and storage-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L32s_v2 is an LSv2 virtual machine with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 8008 GB local storage, optimized for database and storage workloads.
tagline: A storage-dense virtual machine featuring massive local NVMe capacity and balanced multi-core performance for data-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7551 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB RAM
Massive 8008 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average multi-core and database performance with 18.21 million Redis SET ops/sec
Weak single-core and single-threaded compression performance tradeoffs
Best suited for large databases, big data analytics, and high-capacity caching
